overview: Domain Zoo is a Modern aggressive archetype that leverages the Domain mechanic to quickly cast Scion of Draco alongside Leyline of the Guildpact. It uses cheap, high-power creatures like Wild Nacatl and Territorial Kavu to apply early pressure, while the enchantment combo transforms its threats into nearly unblockable forces. The deck relies on a fast start and attrition, adapting its sideboard to a broad metagame.
win_conditions: Attacking with cheap, high-power creatures like Wild Nacatl and Ragavan
Using the Scion of Draco and Leyline of the Guildpact combo to buff creatures into unblockable threats
Clearing blockers with Lightning Bolt and Tribal Flames to deal direct damage
Winning with Tribal Flames after a few attacks

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (fetches/shocks preferred for consistency), at least one Domain enabler (Leyline of the Guildpact, cheap creatures, or graveyard-filling spells), and a mix of early threats (1-2 mana) and interaction.
Specifically, keep hands with Tribal Flames + a cheap creature, or Archon of Cruelty + two cheap spells.
Prioritize hands that can cast a spell on turn 1 to start building graveyard diversity.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan on all-lands, all-removal, or hands missing both early pressure and Domain triggers.

early_game: Play cheap creatures like Wild Nacatl and Ragavan to establish early pressure
Fetch Savai Triome and cycle Shock Lands to establish Domain quickly
Cast Leyline of the Guildpact or use Stubborn Denial as early interaction
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a 1-mana spell (creature or instant) to begin accumulating card types in the graveyard.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast a 2-mana threat or use Tribal Flames if your graveyard has 3+ different card types.
Sequence cheap spells to maximize Domain triggers without overcommitting mana.
Prioritize lands that enter untapped, but never skip casting a crucial spell just to untap a land if you can cast on the current turn.
mid_game: Cast Scion of Draco to synergize with Leyline and buff existing creatures
Attack with Territorial Kavu and Nishoba Brawler, leveraging +1/+1 counters and trample
Use Lightning Bolt and Tribal Flames to clear blockers or finish the opponent
prioritize casting spells that add new types (lands, creatures, instants, sorceries, enchantments) to maximize Tribal Flames and other Domain effects.
Avoid overcommitting mana on low-impact spells when a higher-impact Domain trigger is available.
Manage hand size by using cheap spells to dig or discard dead cards, and preserve interaction for critical turns.
Commit threats when you have interaction or a clear path to the face.
Hold back if the opponent has a board wipe or hard removal pending.
late_game: Rely on attrition and sideboard hosers like Drannith Magistrate or Rest in Peace to stall
Use targeted removal like Prismatic Ending or Pick Your Poison to handle specific threats
Continue applying pressure with buffed creatures until lethal
Archon of Cruelty with multiple +1/+1 counters, Tribal Flames for direct damage or clearing a final blocker, and a swarm of Domain-boosted creatures.
The goal is to pressure while keeping interaction ready for the last turn, often using Tribal Flames to swing for lethal or remove a key defender.
Go all-in when you have a clear path to the face, the opponent has no hard removal or board wipes in hand, or when you can win before their combo/control piece resolves.
If you have 3+ Domain types in your graveyard and a Tribal Flames, it's often worth swinging.
Commit when your opponent is tapped out or has no interaction left.

key_cards.name: Wild Nacatl
key_cards.notes: Face of the archetype; negligible body but interacts perfectly with the strategy

key_cards.name: Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer
key_cards.notes: Generates value, carries games alone, and forces opponent resource commitment

key_cards.name: Territorial Kavu
key_cards.notes: 5/5 for two mana; filters hand or hates graveyard; synergizes heavily with Scion/Leyline

key_cards.name: Nishoba Brawler
key_cards.notes: Vulnerable Territorial Kavu variant that adds consistency and trample

key_cards.name: Scion of Draco
key_cards.notes: Transforms creatures into keyword soup threats; evasive on its own

key_cards.name: Leyline of the Guildpact
key_cards.notes: Facilitates any-color mana access and massively buffs Scion's creatures

key_cards.name: Lightning Bolt
key_cards.notes: Mandatory for clearing blockers and closing out games

key_cards.name: Tribal Flames
key_cards.notes: Forces unfavorable trades and can win games after a few attacks

key_cards.name: Leyline Binding
key_cards.notes: Handles any permanent without hindering the clock; can be sequenced with creatures

key_cards.name: Stubborn Denial
key_cards.notes: Default answer to unwanted spells; acts as Negate or Force Spike against key threats like Blood Moon

sequencing_tips: Fetch Savai Triome on turn one and Breeding Pool on turn two to cast Scion of Draco by turn two
Sequence Leyline Binding with a creature to maximize efficiency without stalling the clock
Use Fetch Lands to find at least one basic land to avoid being two-for-one'd by opponent's Pick Your Poison
Hold Stubborn Denial as a Force Spike against early targeted hate like Blood Moon or key spells
Common mistake: Overlooking graveyard card types when casting spells.
Always count before casting Tribal Flames to ensure maximum value.
Another: Casting Archon of Cruelty too early without a way to pump it or protect it.
Unique interaction: Leyline of the Guildpact counts as an enchantment for Domain, so having it in play or graveyard helps.
Domain triggers check graveyard, library, hand, and battlefield simultaneously.
Remember that fetch lands can be sacrificed for Domain if needed, but usually you want them in library.

matchups.advice: Sideboard in Drannith Magistrate to lock the game alongside Scion/Leyline, and use Flusterstorm as a cheap, uninteractable answer.
Use Cursed Totem to delay opponent's turns and Rest in Peace to block infinite loops.
Sideboard in Rest in Peace to significantly delay the combo and protect your board.
Use Prismatic Ending for low-cost threats and Legion's End for tokens or larger creatures.
Consider sideboarding Doorkeeper Thrull if this archetype grows in the metagame.
Use Tear Asunder for instant-speed artifact/enchantment removal, especially if the game extends.

Use Tribal Flames as a free spell (instant speed, flexible targeting) to pressure through counters. Wait for end step or use cheap spells to force counters. Play around counters by having multiple threats in hand and using fetch lands to dig for answers. Don't overcommit to a single spell; spread your threats across turns.

Apply early pressure to beat them out. Use Tribal Flames to remove their ramp targets or finish them. Don't trade inefficiently; focus on the face. Bring in more direct damage and clock. If they stabilize, use Archon of Cruelty to outvalue them in the late game.

Stabilize with cheap removal (Tribal Flames, pathfinders) and early threats. Don't overextend; use interaction to clear key blockers. Lean into your speed; Domain Zoo is often faster than traditional aggro. Use your five-color mana base to answer their threats while applying your own pressure.

Pressure relentlessly with cheap threats. Use Tribal Flames to clear blockers or swing for lethal. Don't give them time to draw; play around their counterspells by having multiple threats. If they have a wipe, hold up interaction or swing through it. Force them to use resources on your threats rather than their game plan.

It's a race and a resource war. Prioritize graveyard diversity to maximize Tribal Flames. Track their hand size and graveyard. Use discard or cheap interaction to disrupt their setup. Archon of Cruelty wins the long game; pump it with their creatures or your own. Win the graveyard war by casting more spells and keeping your types diverse.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes (especially against control), hard removal (pathfinders, swords), and combo pieces (Storm counters, Amulet targets).

overview_evidence.evidence_span: an Aggro deck that has gained popularity in recent weeks with the "combo" between Leyline of the Guildpact and Scion of Draco!

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Wild Nacatl is basically the face of this archetype... remains one of the main aggressive drops available in Modern
Leyline of the Guildpact... makes Scion of Draco transform his creatures into almost unbeatable threats
Lightning Bolt... offers ways to remove blockers to pressure the opponent, in addition to taking the last points of damage to them
Tribal Flames as removal, as well as being capable of winning games after a few attacks

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: search for Savai Triome on the first turn and Breeding Pool on the second. It offers significant advantages to your creatures if it stays in play
possibility of casting it very early or sequencing it with a creature
look for at least one land with them to avoid taking a two-for-one from Pick Your Poison
using it as a Force Spike for key spells and/or targeted hate, like Blood Moon

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Drannith Magistrate is essential against Cascade decks
Cursed Totem deals with Golgari Yawgmoth
Rest in Peace significantly delays Living End
Prismatic Ending is our answer to Midranges and Aggro with low-cost threats, as well as resolving other problematic permanents like Amulet of Vigor
Legion’s End is an efficient removal against Crashing Footfalls tokens
Path to Exile is our standard one-of answer against larger creatures like Murktide Regent or Primeval Titan
Pick Your Poison has become a necessity for dealing with Scion of Draco or Leyline of the Guildpact , as well as responding to Blood Moon
Doorkeeper Thrull if Indomitable Creativity decks continue to grow
